text:
Lead with the grounded floor: "Citizenship for your agents" + host-neutral durable identity + cross-host/cross-device awareness for your own fleet (Product A, Rung 0–1) — this is real, tested, and matches tenet #9 (no dashboard) and #3 (single-player value day one). Use the vision/society material (multi-app agents, coordination, cross-person) as clearly-labeled "where this is headed," not as present-tense claims — the docs' own authors were careful to mark that boundary and copy should preserve it. [...] - **Honesty guardrails for the copy** (from docs-vision): the beachhead persona is officially an *open question*; the multi-app "society" (todo agent, generative podcast agent) and all cross-person features are *aspirational/unbuilt*; the whole product-spec is draft. The README must lead present-tense with the grounded floor and clearly label the vision as horizon. [...] This tightens my earlier read: anchor on **"citizenship for your agents"** — durable identity + shared awareness for the agents you already run — with the live cross-host `@mention` injection as the concrete hero proof. [...] Far vision imagery (vision.md, agent-society.md): every app exposes an agent with a role (todo agent, podcast agent that *generates* episodes, coding agents); agents self-organize, no central orchestrator; "your todo agent coordinates with your wife's todo agent." This is explicitly aspirational/illustrative, not a committed roadmap item — no dates, no build plan, "phasing as direction, not a build plan" (roadmap.md:3). [...] - The whole todo-agent/podcast-agent multi-app society vignette (vision.md, agent-society.md) — presented as "a real, running example (not hypothetical)" but I could not find any corroborating evidence in README/AGENTS/architecture docs that a todo-app agent or podcast-generation capability actually exists and talks to tenex-edge today. The podcast-*player* agent's existence on relay.tenex.chat is separately claimed in ecosystem.md as real/shipping — but "podcast agent generates new episodes on demand" and "todo agent organizes your day" read as the aspirational extrapolation layered on top of a real shipping podcast *player*. Flag this distinction clearly in any copy: podcast-player-agent-exists ≠ generative-podcast-agent-exists.
- Cross-person / Product B (trusted circle, spouse's agent, "ask Calle's agent") — explicitly gated, "does not ship until" three unmet preconditions (trust-and-safety.md). Zero evidence of implementation. [...] - **Honesty guardrails for the copy** (from docs-vision): the beachhead persona is officially an *open question*; the multi-app "society" (todo agent, generative podcast agent) and all cross-person features are *aspirational/unbuilt*; the whole product-spec is draft. The README must lead present-tense with the grounded floor and clearly label the vision as horizon. [...] - **The shipped reality is more concrete than I expected.** 374 passing tests; the standout *working* mechanism is session-targeted `@mentions` that get **injected as real pasted turns into another agent's live terminal pane, host-neutrally** (Claude Code ↔ Codex ↔ opencode). That's a demonstrable "so what" — and crucially it's the *awareness/messaging floor*, not the coordination/locking ceiling the doctrine warns against. [...] This tightens my earlier read: anchor on **"citizenship for your agents"** — durable identity + shared awareness for the agents you already run — with the live cross-host `@mention` injection as the concrete hero proof.
